chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI Cr. B. A. No.2141 of 2025 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATUREs OF JUDGEs For hearing of bail application ----------------------------------------------------- 11.02.2026 Mr. Sharafuddin Kanhar, A.P.G. & Ms. Rukhsana Qassim Mirjat, ADPP Complainant Kashif Akhtar present ------------------------------------------- Applicant/accused Muhammad Rashid son of Muhammad Shafi seeks pre-arrest bail in FIR No.312/2025, registered at P.S. Khawaja Ajmer Nagri for offence under Sections 402, 406, PPC, after rejection of his bail plea by learned Additional Sessions Judge-VI, Karachi Central vide order dated 12.08.2025. During pendency of instant criminal bail application, parties have settled their dispute out the Court and the complainant of this case has filed application for compromise and has sworn such affidavit. Operative part of compromise application is reproduced as under: “(i) That, it is fact that with reference of Applicant being Agent, the Complainant got purchased the Vehicle bearing No.AZP-429 Toyotta Car through Muhammad Siddiq @ Malik who brought the Car at Showroom of Rashid (Applicant) alongwith Original Complete File, and before dealings, the Complainant inquired the Vehicle physically, via documentary and through C.P.L.C. by making Call and Online. It is fact that at that time the Vehicle were cleared and the dealing for sale and purchase were finalized at Rs.17,55,000/- and the same were paid by the Complainant and thereafter the peaceful possession of aforementioned Car were handed over to the Complainant in presence of Rashid/Applicant.” Complainant is present and reiterated the facts mentioned in the compromise application along with its supporting affidavit.

Operative part of compromise application is reproduced as under: “(i) That, it is fact that with reference of Applicant being Agent, the Complainant got purchased the Vehicle bearing No.AZP-429 Toyotta Car through Muhammad Siddiq @ Malik who brought the Car at Showroom of Rashid (Applicant) alongwith Original Complete File, and before dealings, the Complainant inquired the Vehicle physically, via documentary and through C.P.L.C. by making Call and Online. It is fact that at that time the Vehicle were cleared and the dealing for sale and purchase were finalized at Rs.17,55,000/- and the same were paid by the Complainant and thereafter the peaceful possession of aforementioned Car were handed over to the Complainant in presence of Rashid/Applicant.” Complainant is present and reiterated the facts mentioned in the compromise application along with its supporting affidavit. In view of above, interim pre-arrest bail granted to the applicant/accused by this Court vide order dated 21.08.2025 is confirmed on the same terms and conditions on the basis of affidavit sworn by the complainant. J U D G E Gulsher/PS
